Notice Of Motion; Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings; Memorandum Of Points Of Authorities
Off Calendar Jun-05-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (via Zoom):

Jesse Valencia, Esq., ERSKINE LAW GROUP, APC, (949) 777-6032, for Defendant.

No other appearances.

Defendant GENERAL MOTORS LLC's motion for judgment on the pleadings on the fourth cause of action for fraud/concealment.

Ruling - A R G U E D. Upon conclusion of argument, the court adopts the following tentative ruling: Defendant's motion for judgment on the pleadings on the fourth cause of action for fraud/concealment is denied. The court accepts plaintiff's late-filed opposition. Defendant was not prejudiced and substantively responded to the late filing. Plaintiff alleges a sufficient relationship between plaintiff and defendant for the concealment claim. (Dhital v. Nissan N. Am. Inc. (2022) 84 Cal.App.5th 828, 844 [allegations that plaintiff purchased vehicle from Nissan dealership, that Nissan issued a warranty, and dealership was agent of Nissan were sufficient to show relationship with manufacturer for concealment claim] (review granted).) Plaintiff's active concealment and exclusive knowledge allegations are sufficient to support the concealment cause of action. (Heliotis v. Schuman (1986) 181 Cal.App.3d 646, 651 [party may plead concealment claim based on active concealment or exclusive knowledge where no fiduciary relationship exists]; Complaint, pars. 61-78; 120-140.) The court notes that specificity requirement for a fraud/concealment claim is "relaxed when it is apparent from the allegations that the defendant necessarily possesses knowledge of the facts." (Quelimane Co. v. Steward Title Guaranty Co. (1998) 19 Cal.4th 26, 47; see also Alfaro v. Community Housing Imp. System & Planning Ass'n, Inc. (2009) 171 Cal.App.4th 1356, 1384.) A factual issue remains whether defendant's alleged knowledge of transmission defects in other vehicles shows scienter with respect to plaintiff's vehicle.

Counsel for Defendant is required to prepare a proposed form of order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B, Ulmer Jr., Clerk: R. Woods, Not Reported. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion; Motion To Strike Punitive Damages From Plaintiffs' Complaint; Memorandum Of Points Of Authorities
Off Calendar Jun-05-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom):

Jesse Valencia, Esq., ERSKINE LAW GROUP, APC, (949) 777-6032, for Defendant.

No other appearances.

GENERAL MOTORS LLC's Motion To Strike Punitive Damages From Plaintiffs' Complaint.

Ruling - A R G U E D. Upon conclusion of argument, the court adopts the following tentative ruling:

Defendant's motion to strike is denied. The concealment cause of action supports the request for exemplary damages.

Counsel for Defendant is required to prepare a proposed form of order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with crc 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B, Ulmer Jr., Clerk: R. Woods, Not Reported. (302/RBU)
